svg.text: • Adjacency list — Map<Node, List<Neighbour>> — sparse graphs, default choice
• Adjacency matrix — bool[n][n] — dense graphs, O(1) edge query
• Edge list — List<(u, v, w)> — Kruskal MST, Bellman-Ford
• Implicit — grid neighbours, word-ladder transformations, bit-flip moves
Edge weights, directionality, and cycle structure are the three axes
of graph problems. Pick the algorithm to match.
• Reachability / connected components — BFS or DFS or Union-Find
• Shortest path, negative weights — Bellman-Ford or SPFA
• All-pairs shortest path — Floyd-Warshall (O(V³)) for small V
• Topological order / cycle detection on DAG — Kahn or DFS post-order
Identify the shape; the algorithm follows.
